# UC-13.2.4 · Entity Status Monitoring

## Description

Entity health provides granular visibility into individual infrastructure components feeding services. Unstable entities degrade service health.

## Value

Entity health provides granular visibility into individual infrastructure components feeding services. Unstable entities degrade service health.

## Implementation

Configure entity discovery (AD, CMDB, cloud APIs). Monitor entity states (active, inactive, unstable). Alert when critical entities become inactive. Track entity population for coverage analysis.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk ITSI.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: ITSI entity overview, entity health scores.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Configure entity discovery (AD, CMDB, cloud APIs). Monitor entity states (active, inactive, unstable). Alert when critical entities become inactive. Track entity population for coverage analysis.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| inputlookup itsi_entities
| where entity_status!="active"
| table title, entity_type, entity_status, last_seen
```

Understanding this SPL

**Entity Status Monitoring** — Entity health provides granular visibility into individual infrastructure components feeding services. Unstable entities degrade service health.

Documented **Data sources**: ITSI entity overview, entity health scores. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk ITSI. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Loads rows via `inputlookup` (KV store or CSV lookup) for enrichment or reporting.
• Filters the current rows with `where entity_status!="active"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **Entity Status Monitoring**): table title, entity_type, entity_status, last_seen


Step 3 — Validate
Run the search when you know the service or episode context. Compare with the ITSI Service Analyzer, episode or notable views, and Glass Tables or drill-downs for the same time range.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Status grid (entities by type × status), Table (inactive entities), Single value (active entity count).

## SPL

```spl
| inputlookup itsi_entities
| where entity_status!="active"
| table title, entity_type, entity_status, last_seen
```

## Visualization

Status grid (entities by type × status), Table (inactive entities), Single value (active entity count).
